scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a CPG-specific insights brief with explicit metric logic, source lineage, caveats, and action thresholds.
Ambiguous denominator handling blocked publication until the source hierarchy and metric definition were resolved.
Stale inventory and hold extracts triggered a low-confidence escalation instead of a smoothed trend narrative.
Cross-system service-level disagreement was preserved as a reconciliation problem rather than collapsed into one value.
Unsupported causal language was rejected and the output stayed inside descriptive analysis.
Unsupported disclosure language was escalated with named evidence gaps and without false certainty.
Lot-level conflict across MES, WMS, and QMS was treated as a traceability blocker and not downgraded into noise.
Held inventory was not counted as saleable supply and quality-release authority remained with adjacent owners.
Supplier substitution equivalence was refused and routed to procurement and supplier-quality specialists.
The lane rejected rework and release approval requests while preserving the evidence package for handoff.
Missing tenant metric dictionaries, release-authority context, and source precedence produced a blocked-on-prerequisites response.
Tenant-specific metric adaptation was recorded explicitly and the public baseline remained visible instead of silently replaced.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to CPG consumer-insights analysis grounded in lot, inventory, supplier, hold, and release evidence.
Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all analytics-reporting-and-disclosure abilities, and all manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities required by the issue.
